# Gangjeong Peace Music Camp (강정피스앤뮤직캠프)

> An anti-war peace music festival held annually in Gangjeong Village, Jeju Island, South Korea. Founded in 2023. Musicians from diverse genres gather to sing for peace in conflict zones worldwide. Free admission.

## Key Facts
- Official Name: Gangjeong Peace Music Camp (강정피스앤뮤직캠프)
- Location: Gangjeong Sports Park, Seogwipo, Jeju Special Self-Governing Province, South Korea
- Founded: 2023 (1st camp: June 10, 2023, 11 music acts)
- 2nd Camp: June 14, 2025 (11 music acts)
- 3rd Camp: June 5-7, 2026 (54 music acts, expanded to 3-day format)
- Album: "To You in a Distant Place Whose Name I Don't Know" (이름을 모르는 먼 곳의 그대에게, released October 12, 2024, 12 tracks)
- Slogan: "Let's sing, let's dance, end the war!" (노래하자, 춤추자, 전쟁을 끝내자!)
- Free admission with voluntary donations
- Languages supported: Korean, English, Japanese, Chinese (Simplified/Traditional), Spanish, French, German, Portuguese, Russian, Arabic, Hindi, Indonesian

## Pages
- Home: https://peaceandmusic.net
- Camp 2026: https://peaceandmusic.net/camps/2026
- Camp 2025: https://peaceandmusic.net/camps/2025
- Camp 2023: https://peaceandmusic.net/camps/2023
- Album About: https://peaceandmusic.net/album/about
- Musicians: https://peaceandmusic.net/album/musicians
- Tracks: https://peaceandmusic.net/album/tracks
- Gallery: https://peaceandmusic.net/gallery
- Press: https://peaceandmusic.net/press
- Videos: https://peaceandmusic.net/videos

## Camp 2026 Timetable (3rd Gangjeong Peace Music Camp)

The 3rd Gangjeong Peace Music Camp runs three consecutive days. Each performance is 25 minutes, separated by 5–10 minute transitions. Admission is free.

- Day 1 — Friday, June 5, 2026 · 17:00–22:57 KST · 12 acts · https://peaceandmusic.net/camps/2026#timetable-day-2026-06-05
- Day 2 — Saturday, June 6, 2026 · 12:00–22:37 KST · 21 acts · https://peaceandmusic.net/camps/2026#timetable-day-2026-06-06
- Day 3 — Sunday, June 7, 2026 · 11:00–21:30 KST · 21 acts · https://peaceandmusic.net/camps/2026#timetable-day-2026-06-07

Each individual performance has a deep-link anchor in the form `#act-YYYY-MM-DD-N` (e.g. `#act-2026-06-05-3` is the 3rd act on day 1). Performer profiles live at `https://peaceandmusic.net/camps/2026/musicians/{id}`.

Participating artists span Korean indie, folk, blues, rock, ambient, electronic, and world music — including Yun Sunae (윤선애), Jeong Jinseok (정진석), Choi Sangdon (최상돈), Kim Dongsan & Blue Neighbors, Dear Arcadian, Honey Whiskey, Materials Pound, Dog Last Page, Taehyeon (태히언), HANASH, TAGI, Rainbow99, Sabbaha (사바하), Meridies (메리디에스), and 40+ others.

## Structured Data (schema.org)

Every camp page exposes JSON-LD for:
- `MusicEvent` for the full festival plus 54 individual `MusicEvent` subEvents (one per performance, each linked to the performer's profile and tagged with start/end time in KST)
- `FAQPage` with 6 common questions (when/where/free/support/lineup/schedule)
- `BreadcrumbList`, `ItemList` (ordered timetable), `HowTo` (attendance steps), `WebPage`, `Organization`, `WebSite`

Each musician profile page exposes `ProfilePage` schema with genre, social links, and organization affiliation.

## Context
Gangjeong Village in Jeju Island has been the site of nearly 20 years of peace resistance since the 2007 designation of a naval base construction site. Despite 94% resident opposition, the Jeju Naval Base was built, destroying the UNESCO-protected Gureombi Rock in 2012. The village received the IPB Sean MacBride Peace Award in 2015, recognized globally for its nonviolent peace movement. Residents have maintained daily peace prayers for over 7,000 consecutive days.

The Gangjeong Peace Music Camp was born from this movement. It brings together musicians across genres — rock, folk, jazz, electronic, ambient, world music — to stand in solidarity with conflict zones globally, including Palestine, Ukraine, and the Korean Peninsula.
